query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "geometric action model for robot policy learning" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (d) as it directly addresses the "Geometric Action Model" mechanism for robot policy learning by proposing a geometry-grounded video world model (GEM-4D) that explicitly injects geometric supervision to improve action execution, serving as a canonical follow-up or foundational reference for this specific research direction.
